text.title: AWS IaC Templates Core Rules
text.summary: 5+ testable rules grounding the methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale. Includes the skip-this-methodology rule that the decision-tree maps to.

rule.statement: Stateful resources (RDS, S3, EBS, EFS, DynamoDB) MUST default to encryption-at-rest with a customer-managed KMS key in production templates. Encryption disabled requires explicit `encryption_disabled_rationale` parameter set by the operator.
rule.rationale: Default-encrypted resources prevent accidental plaintext storage. KMS rotation + IAM auditability are baseline SOC2 / ISO27001 controls.

rule.statement: IAM roles in templates MUST follow least-privilege: explicit resource ARNs (no `Resource: '*'`), specific actions (no `Action: '*'`), permission boundaries on team-created roles. Wildcard policies are blocked by `cfn-lint` / `tflint` rules.

rationale.source: AWS Security Best Practices; OWASP Top 10 cloud

rule.rationale: Wildcard IAM grants are the #1 cause of cross-service privilege escalation in AWS. Permission boundaries cap the maximum effective permissions even when a team creates over-broad role policies.

rule.statement: Stateful resources MUST set `deletion_protection = true` and `prevent_destroy = true` in production templates. Stack-level `DeletionPolicy: Retain` for RDS, DynamoDB, S3 buckets that store user data.

rationale.source: AWS RDS docs; Terraform lifecycle docs

rule.rationale: Accidental `terraform destroy` or stack-delete on production stateful resources causes irreversible data loss. Deletion protection forces a deliberate two-step removal.

rule.statement: Every resource MUST carry mandatory tags: `Project`, `Environment`, `Owner`, `CostCenter`, `ManagedBy`. Templates use a `local.common_tags` map; resources merge that map into their tags block.
rule.rationale: Untagged resources break cost attribution, ownership lookup, and incident response. Mandatory tags enforced at template level prevent the `we'll-tag-it-later` debt.

rule.statement: Apply the templates to one stack / module per pass. Multi-pattern stacks (VPC + ECS + RDS in one template) MUST be split into composable modules. Skip when the workload mismatches every template — fork the pattern, do not force-fit.

rationale.source: F-066 content-completion; Terraform module composition pattern

rule.rationale: Anti-conflation: monolith templates hide blast radius and slow change-set review. Force-fitting a template guarantees hardened defaults work against the workload.
